summary: The review of the Material Transfer Agreement (MTA Example 1.pdf) indicates a generally moderate risk posture, with most flagged clauses falling outside the scope of the University of Auckland's Preferred Contracting Positions or Standard Templates. Key issues include liability limitations and indemnities (clauses 4, 7, and 8), data security and privacy obligations (clauses 5, 6, and 15), and governing law and jurisdiction (clause 24). The recommended next step is to escalate amber and red-flagged clauses to the appropriate internal stakeholders, such as the Risk Office, General Counsel, or Auckland UniServices, for further review and alignment with institutional policies.

flags.clause_title: 8. The Recipient Institution shall indemnify the Providing Institution, the
flags.snippet: 8. The Recipient Institution shall indemnify the Providing Institution, the Providing Institution’s employees in full against all liability, loss, damages or expenses (including legal and other professional fees and expenses) from or in connection with the handling, storage or disposal of the Material and/or Data by or on behalf of the University.
flags.rationale: This clause conflicts with UoA Position #POS-07 on Liability Limitations and Exclusions. The clause imposes an uncapped indemnity obligation ('in full against all liability') on the Recipient Institution, which is inconsistent with the preferred position that total liability (including indemnities) must be capped at the lesser of NZD$500,000 or the contract value. Additionally, the clause does not explicitly exclude consequential or indirect losses, nor does it limit liability to direct losses caused by negligent acts/omissions or wilful misconduct. Escalation to the Research Grants and Contracts Delegations is required to address the liability cap and indemnity scope.
flags.text:
8. The Recipient Institution shall indemnify the Providing Institution, the Providing
Institution’s employees in full against all liability, loss, damages or expenses
(including legal and other professional fees and expenses) from or in connection
with the handling, storage or disposal of the Material and/or Data by or on behalf
of the University.

flags.clause_title: 4. The Recipient Institution understands that the Material may have hazardous
flags.snippet: 4. The Recipient Institution understands that the Material may have hazardous properties, contain infectious agents or pose other health and safety risks. Subject to clause 12, the Provider Institution makes no represen…
flags.rationale: This clause touches on liability limitations and exclusions, which are sensitive under UoA Position #POS-07. While the clause excludes liability for the Provider Institution and disclaims warranties, it does not explicitly align with UoA's preferred or acceptable positions regarding liability caps or exclusions of indirect/consequential losses. The clause does not specify a monetary cap for liability, nor does it address exclusions for indirect losses explicitly. This deviation requires confirmation against the Research Contract Delegations and may need escalation to the Portfolio Manager for Trusted Funders or the Risk Office for liability cap assessment.
flags.text:
4. The Recipient Institution understands that the Material may have hazardous
properties, contain infectious agents or pose other health and safety risks. Subject
to clause 12, the Provider Institution makes no representations and gives no
warranties either express or implied in relation to the Material: for example
(without limitation), no warranties are given about quality or fitness for a particular
purpose, or freedom from infection of the Material. The Provider Institution will not
be liable for any use made of the Material by the Recipient Institution. The Recipient
Institution will use the Material in accordance with good laboratory practice
standards, all due skill and care and with dignity, sensitivity and respect. The
Recipient Institution warrants that it will only use, or permit the use of the Material
and Data in work that has ethical approval, as stated in Appendix 1.

flags.clause_title: 5. Recipient shall take appropriate measures to ensure a level of security
flags.snippet: 5. Recipient shall take appropriate measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including amongst other things, as appropriate: i. the encryption of the Data; ii. the ability to ensure the on-going confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of systems and services for managing and storing the Data; iii. the ability to restore the availability and access to the Data in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident; and iv. having and implementing a process for regularly testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the management and storage of the Data.
flags.rationale: This clause addresses data security measures, which indirectly relate to confidentiality obligations under UoA Position #POS-11. While the clause does not explicitly conflict with UoA's preferred or acceptable confidentiality positions, it introduces additional obligations regarding encryption, system resilience, and testing that are not covered in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions. These measures are reasonable but require confirmation to ensure alignment with UoA's confidentiality standards. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts is recommended for review.
flags.text:
5. Recipient shall take appropriate measures to ensure a level of security appropriate
to the risk, including amongst other things, as appropriate:
i. the encryption of the Data;
ii. the ability to ensure the on-going confidentiality, integrity, availability and
resilience of systems and services for managing and storing the Data;
2 Applicable Laws means all laws, rules, regulations, codes of practice, research governance or ethical
guidelines, or other requirements of any Regulatory Authority, that may apply to the use of the Material by the
Recipient Institution from time to time, including (but not limited) the Human Tissue Act 2004 or the Human Tissue
(Scotland)_Act 2006, the Human Tissue (Quality and Safety for Human Application) Regulations 2007, the Human
Fertilisation and Embryology Act 1990 (as amended), the EU Tissues and Cells Directive (2004/23/EC) and
Commission Directives 2006/17/EC and 2006/86/EC. The Human Tissue Authority Directions and Codes of
Practice, and the Medicines for Human Use (Clinical Trials) Regulations 2004, as updated and amended from time
to time and, where relevant, the national implementations of the same.
Page 2 of 8

iii. the ability to restore the availability and access to the Data in a timely manner
in the event of a physical or technical incident; and
iv. having and implementing a process for regularly testing, assessing and
evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for
ensuring the security of the management and storage of the Data.

flags.clause_title: 7. Except to the extent prohibited by law and subject to clause 12, the Recipient
flags.snippet: 7. Except to the extent prohibited by law and subject to clause 12, the Recipient Institution assumes all liability for damages which may arise from its receipt, use, storage or disposal of the Material and/or Data. The maximum aggregate liability of the Recipient Institution to the Provider in relation to this Agreement, including any under indemnity, is limited to an amount equal to NZD 100,000 but only to the extent that such liability can be limited in law and such limitation shall not apply to any liability arising from the Recipient Institution’s wilful misconduct or gross negligence, or any misuse of the Material or Data.
flags.rationale: The clause limits the Recipient Institution's liability to NZD 100,000, which is well below the NZD$500,000 threshold in UoA's Preferred Position for liability caps. While the clause includes an 'assumes all liability' phrasing, the monetary cap significantly mitigates exposure, making this clause acceptable under UoA Position #POS-07. The carve-outs for wilful misconduct, gross negligence, and misuse align with acceptable exceptions under the Preferred Position. Escalation is not required as the liability cap is within acceptable limits.
flags.text:
7. Except to the extent prohibited by law and subject to clause 12, the Recipient
Institution assumes all liability for damages which may arise from its receipt, use,
storage or disposal of the Material and/or Data. The maximum aggregate liability
of the Recipient Institution to the Provider in relation to this Agreement, including
any under indemnity, is limited to an amount equal to NZD 100,000 but only to the
extent that such liability can be limited in law and such limitation shall not apply to
any liability arising from the Recipient Institution’s wilful misconduct or gross
negligence, or any misuse of the Material or Data. The Provider Institution will not
be liable to the Recipient Institution for any loss, claim or demand made by the
Recipient Institution, or made against the Recipient Institution by any other party,
due to or arising from its use, storage or disposal of the Material and/or Data by the
Recipient Institution, except to the extent the law otherwise requires.

flags.clause_title: 14. Any results generated in the course of the Study from the Recipient’s use of
flags.snippet: 14. Any results generated in the course of the Study from the Recipient’s use of the Materials and/or Data shall be jointly owned by the Parties. Each Party may use the results for its own teaching and non-commercial re…
flags.rationale: The clause addresses Intellectual Property (IP) ownership and joint use of results, which falls under UoA Position #POS-IP. UoA Contracting Positions explicitly state that all IP clauses must be referred to Auckland UniServices (Head of IP) for review, as UoA does not set IP policy independently. This clause must be escalated to UniServices for assessment.
flags.text:
14. Any results generated in the course of the Study from the Recipient’s use of the
Materials and/or Data shall be jointly owned by the Parties. Each Party may use the
results for its own teaching and non-commercial research purposes, provided that
such use is subject to the prior written consent of the other Party. Each Party retains
ownership of all rights (including Intellectual Property Rights) in its information,
materials and other items existing prior to or developed independently of this
Agreement.

flags.clause_title: 17. The Provider Institution has the right to terminate this agreement forthwith at
flags.snippet: 17. The Provider Institution has the right to terminate this agreement forthwith at any time by means of written notice to Recipient Institution if the ethical approval is withdrawn or if the Recipient Institution is in breach of this Agreement.
flags.rationale: This clause addresses termination rights, which align partially with UoA Position #POS-19. While it includes termination for breach and ethical approval withdrawal, it does not provide mutual two-way termination or address termination costs and uncancellable obligations, which are part of the Preferred tier. The clause is acceptable for this type of agreement but requires confirmation by the Contract Manager to ensure alignment with UoA's termination standards. Escalation to the Senior Manager Research Grants and Contracts may be required for further review.
flags.text:
17. The Provider Institution has the right to terminate this agreement forthwith at any
time by means of written notice to Recipient Institution if the ethical approval is
withdrawn or if the Recipient Institution is in breach of this Agreement. In the case
of termination or expiration of this Agreement, the Recipient Institution shall
immediately discontinue all use of the Material and Data, and at the Provider
Ins titution's discretion, promptly Preatguer n4, orfe 8t ain, transfer or destroy all unused

Material and Data, and provide written confirmation that this has been completed. If requested, the Recipient Institution must certify that it has complied in full with any such requirement of the Provider Institution. Should an individual donor or their next of kin rescind their consent, the Provider Institution will require and the Recipient Institution agrees to discontinue using the appropriately identified sample and return or destroy it in accordance with the Provider Institution's instructions.

flags.clause_title: 24. This Agreement shall be governed by English Law, and the English Courts shall
flags.snippet: 24. This Agreement shall be governed by English Law, and the English Courts shall have exclusive jurisdiction to deal with any dispute which may arise out of or in connection with this Letter Agreement.
flags.rationale: The clause specifies that the Agreement is governed by English Law and grants exclusive jurisdiction to the English Courts. This falls under the UoA-sensitive topic of Governing Law and Jurisdiction. According to UoA Position #POS-16, the preferred governing law is New Zealand, but the United Kingdom is listed as acceptable. However, the clause specifies 'exclusive jurisdiction,' which is not explicitly addressed in the acceptable tier. This requires confirmation to ensure alignment with UoA's acceptable practices. Escalation to General Counsel is recommended if further clarification is needed.
flags.text: 24. This Agreement shall be governed by English Law, and the English Courts shall have exclusive jurisdiction to deal with any dispute which may arise out of or in connection with this Letter Agreement.

flags.clause_title: 25. This Agreement shall be effective as of the date of the last signature below and
flags.snippet: 25. This Agreement shall be effective as of the date of the last signature below and shall terminate upon termination of the Study in Appendix 1 or 2 years from the effective date, whichever is sooner.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the term and termination of the agreement, which is a UoA-sensitive topic. While it specifies a fixed term (2 years or earlier upon study termination), it does not explicitly address mutual two-way termination for material breach, insolvency, or UoA's right to terminate for non-payment, as required by UoA's Preferred Position (POS-19). However, it does not contradict the Preferred Position outright and could be acceptable depending on the broader contract context. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts, is recommended to confirm alignment with UoA's termination policies.
flags.text: 25. This Agreement shall be effective as of the date of the last signature below and shall terminate upon termination of the Study in Appendix 1 or 2 years from the effective date, whichever is sooner.

flags.clause_title: 1. The Recipient Institution will not use the Material for administration to human
flags.snippet: 1. The Recipient Institution will not use the Material for administration to human subjects or human application as that term is defined in the Human Tissue (Quality and Safety for Human Application) Regulations 2007 (or equivalent as each may be replaced or amended from time to time) or for clinical or diagnostic purposes.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses restrictions on the use of materials for human administration, human application, clinical, or diagnostic purposes. This topic is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of pre-defined policy tiers and is flagged as blue.
flags.text:
1. The Recipient Institution will not use the Material for administration to human
subjects or human application as that term is defined in the Human Tissue (Quality
and Safety for Human Application) Regulations 2007 (or equivalent as each may be
r eplaced or amended from time to time) or for clinical or diagnostic purposes.

flags.clause_title: 2. The Recipient Institution may use the Material and Data solely for the purposes
flags.snippet: 2. The Recipient Institution may use the Material and Data solely for the purposes of the Study and as described in Appendix 1, from the date of receipt of the M2 aterial. The Recipient Institution will comply fully wit…
flags.rationale: The clause addresses compliance with laws, regulations, and ethical guidelines regarding the use, handling, and disposal of materials and data. This topic is not explicitly covered in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of pre-defined positions and templates, warranting a blue flag.
flags.text:
2. The Recipient Institution may use the Material and Data solely for the purposes of
the Study and as described in Appendix 1, from the date of receipt of the M2 aterial.
The Recipient Institution will comply fully with all applicable environmental, health
and safety laws, the Human Tissue Act 2004 and all Applicable Laws , rules,
regulations, codes of conduct, codes of practice, research governance or ethical
guidelines, and/or guidance, directions, decisions, determinations, or other
requirements issued, from time to time, by any regulatory authority, that may apply
with respect to its handling (including, but not limited to, storage, transport, use,
processing, disposal or return) of the Material and all applicable laws, or other
requirements issued, from time to time, by any regulatory authority, that may apply
to the use, processing, storage, transfer and/or disposal of the Data.

flags.clause_title: 3. The Parties shall use a courier with suitable skill and experience to safely
flags.snippet: 3. The Parties shall use a courier with suitable skill and experience to safely transport the Material in accordance with all Applicable Laws. The provider Institution will bear the cost of carriage and any necessary insurance.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the logistics of transporting materials, including courier selection, cost allocation, and risk transfer. This topic is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of pre-defined policy tiers and is flagged as blue.
flags.text:
3. The Parties shall use a courier with suitable skill and experience to safely transport
the Material in accordance with all Applicable Laws. The provider Institution will
bear the cost of carriage and any necessary insurance. The Provider Institution
makes no charge for the Material. Risk in and responsibility for the Material shall
pass to the Recipient Institution once it is received at its premises. If so requested
by the Provider Institution the Recipient Institution shall provide it with written
confirmation of the safe receipt of the Materials promptly after their delivery to the
Recipient Institution’s laboratory.

flags.clause_title: 6. The Recipient shall keep all of the Data secure from any unauthorised or
flags.snippet: 6. The Recipient shall keep all of the Data secure from any unauthorised or accidental use, access, disclosure, damage, loss or destruction. The Recipient will not permit the Data or any part of it to come into the poss…
flags.rationale: The clause addresses data security and management, which is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. No specific policy or template reference exists for this topic.
flags.text:
6. The Recipient shall keep all of the Data secure from any unauthorised or accidental
use, access, disclosure, damage, loss or destruction. The Recipient will not permit
the Data or any part of it to come into the possession or control of any individual
other than those of its employees or students involved in the Research who are
under the direct supervision of the Recipient Scientist. The Recipient shall comply
with any request from the Provider to amend, rectify, transfer, block or destroy any
of the Data.

flags.clause_title: 10. The Recipient Institution agrees to obtain the written consent of the Provider
flags.snippet: 10. The Recipient Institution agrees to obtain the written consent of the Provider Institution if there is any material change to the proposed use of the Material in the Study as described in Appendix 1.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the requirement for written consent from the Provider Institution in case of material changes to the proposed use of the Material. This topic is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of pre-defined policy tiers.
flags.text:
10. The Recipient Institution agrees to obtain the written consent of the Provider
Institution if there is any material change to the proposed use of the Material in the
Study as described in Appendix 1.

flags.clause_title: 12. The Provider Institution undertakes that where required by Applicable Laws the
flags.snippet: 12. The Provider Institution undertakes that where required by Applicable Laws the Material has been obtained from humans with the appropriate consent as required by the Human Tissue Act 2004 and with ethical approval. 
flags.rationale: The clause addresses compliance with the Human Tissue Act 2004 and ethical approval for human-derived materials, as well as ownership and licensing terms for the Materials and Data. This topic is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. Therefore, it falls outside the scope of pre-defined tiers and is flagged as blue.
flags.text:
12. The Provider Institution undertakes that where required by Applicable Laws the
Material has been obtained from humans with the appropriate consent as required
by the Human Tissue Act 2004 and with ethical approval. The Provider Institution
retains ownership or custodian rights in the Materials and Data (and any
unmodified derivatives of the Materials) it provides as part of this Agreement and
hereby grants to the Recipient Institution a non-exclusive, royalty-free licence to
use the Material and Data for the Study only.

flags.clause_title: 15. The Recipient undertakes not to attempt to identify or contact any individual
flags.snippet: 15. The Recipient undertakes not to attempt to identify or contact any individual to whom the Data relates (Data Subject). In the event that the Recipient inadvertently identifies any Data Subject, then it will notify...
flags.rationale: The clause addresses data protection and privacy obligations, specifically prohibiting the identification or contact of individuals related to the data (Data Subjects). This topic is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, the clause falls outside the scope of pre-defined UoA policies and templates, and no escalation route is specified.
flags.text:
15. The Recipient undertakes not to attempt to identify or contact any individual to
whom the Data relates (“Data Subject”). In the event that the Recipient
inadvertently identifies any Data Subject, then it will notify the Provider
immediately setting out in reasonable detail the circumstances by which it
happened and will then comply with all applicable laws that may apply to the use,
processing, storage, transfer and/or disposal of the Data in question, including,
without limitation, the Data Protection Act (2018) and the UK General Data
Protection Regulation (“UK GDPR”) (‘’Data Protection Laws’’), in relation to the use,
processing, storage, transfer and/or disposal of the Data under this Agreement.
Other than such disclosure to the Provider, the Recipient shall not share the
identification of that Data Subject with any other person or attempt to contact the
Data Subject themselves.

flags.clause_title: 16. Nothing included in this Agreement shall prevent the Provider Institution from
flags.snippet: 16. Nothing included in this Agreement shall prevent the Provider Institution from being able to distribute the Material and/or Data to other entities as described in Appendix 1. If, as per the details included in Appen…
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the Provider Institution's ability to distribute Material and/or Data to other entities and assigns compliance responsibility to the Recipient Institution. This topic is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of pre-defined policy tiers and is flagged as blue.
flags.text:
16. Nothing included in this Agreement shall prevent the Provider Institution from
being able to distribute the Material and/or Data to other entities as described in
Appendix 1. If, as per the details included in Appendix 1, the Material and/or Data
is to be transferred to another institution for the purposes of the Study, the
responsibility for compliance with the terms of this Agreement rests with the
Recipient Institution.

flags.clause_title: 19. The Parties agree that this Agreement and any subsequent amendments to it
flags.snippet: 19. The Parties agree that this Agreement and any subsequent amendments to it shall be binding on their successors in title, and assigns, and undertake to take all necessary steps to ensure that they are so bound.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the binding nature of the agreement on successors and assigns, which is not covered by any UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of pre-defined policy and template references.
flags.text: 19. The Parties agree that this Agreement and any subsequent amendments to it shall be binding on their successors in title, and assigns, and undertake to take all necessary steps to ensure that they are so bound.

flags.clause_title: 20. This Agreement does not create any right enforceable by any person not a Party
flags.snippet: 20. This Agreement does not create any right enforceable by any person not a Party to it.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses third-party enforceability, which is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of pre-defined policy or template guidance.
flags.text: 20. This Agreement does not create any right enforceable by any person not a Party to it.

flags.clause_title: 21. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties in
flags.snippet: 21. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties in relation to its subject matter and no statements or representations made by any Party have been relied upon by the other in entering into this Agreement.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the 'entire agreement' concept, which is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of pre-defined policy tiers.
flags.text: 21. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties in relation to its subject matter and no statements or representations made by any Party have been relied upon by the other in entering into this Agreement.

flags.clause_title: 22. The rights and obligations of the Parties are personal and may not be assigned
flags.snippet: 22. The rights and obligations of the Parties are personal and may not be assigned at any time without the prior written consent of the other Party which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld; provided that it shall be a requirement in all cases of assignation that the assignee undertakes to perform all outstanding obligations of the assignor as though the assignee had been an original party hereto.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses assignment of rights and obligations, which is not covered by any UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of pre-defined policy tiers and is flagged as blue.
flags.text: 22. The rights and obligations of the Parties are personal and may not be assigned at any time without the prior written consent of the other Party which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld; provided that it shall be a requirement in all cases of assignation that the assignee undertakes to perform all outstanding obligations of the assignor as though the assignee had been an original party hereto.

This topic is not addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of pre-defined positions and requires specific review for compliance with applicable laws and ethical considerations.
flags.text: The term “Material” means material, other than human gametes or embryos, which consists of, or includes human cells and which is considered “Relevant Material” for the purposes of the Human Tissue Act 2004¹ together with related data.

As such, it falls outside the scope of pre-defined positions and requires independent assessment.
flags.text: The Recipient Institution will hold the Material and Data on the terms of this Agreement and solely for the purpose of the Study as described in Appendix 1, within the research group of [REDACTED] (the “Recipient Scientist”).

flags.clause_title: The Recipient Institution hereby agrees to comply and procure that the Recipient
flags.snippet: The Recipient Institution hereby agrees to comply and procure that the Recipient Scientist and all personnel who work with the Material and Data comply with the following terms and conditions:  The Human Tissue Act 2004 applies to the authorised activities principally the removal, storage and use of Relevant Materials (as defined under the Act, including human cells, tissue and organs, but not cell lines) which come from a living or deceased person for Scheduled Purposes (these are set out in Schedule 1 of the Act, including, but not limited to, research in connection with disorders, or the function of the human body; education or training relating to human health; and transplantation).
flags.rationale: The clause addresses compliance with the Human Tissue Act 2004, which is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of pre-defined policy and template references.
flags.text:
The Recipient Institution hereby agrees to comply and procure that the Recipient Scientist and all personnel who work with the Material and Data comply with the following terms and conditions:  

¹ The Human Tissue Act 2004 applies to the “authorised activities” principally the removal, storage and use of “Relevant Materials” (as defined under the Act, including human cells, tissue and organs, but not cell lines) which come from a living or deceased person for “Scheduled Purposes” (these are set out in Schedule 1 of the Act, including, but not limited to, “research in connection with disorders, or the function of the human body”; “education or training relating to human health”; and “transplantation”).

flags.clause_title: Whereas the Provider Institution and Recipient Institution are collaborating in a
flags.snippet: Whereas the Provider Institution and Recipient Institution are collaborating in a study to explore [REDACTED] (the Study) and Provider Institution now wishes for the Recipient Institution to perform certain analyses/studies as described in Appendix 1.
flags.rationale: The clause is a preamble describing the collaboration and purpose of the study, which is not addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. It does not conflict with any existing policy or template.
flags.text: Whereas the Provider Institution and Recipient Institution are collaborating in a study to explore [REDACTED] (the “Study”) and Provider Institution now wishes for the Recipient Institution to perform certain analyses/studies as described in Appendix 1.

flags.clause_title: Whereas Provider Institution also intends to transfer to the Recipient Institution
flags.snippet: Whereas Provider Institution also intends to transfer to the Recipient Institution data as further described in Appendix 3 (Data) collected as part of the [REDACTED].
flags.rationale: The clause introduces the transfer of data, which is not explicitly addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of the existing policy framework and requires further review to ensure compliance with UoA's data governance and privacy policies.
flags.text: Whereas Provider Institution also intends to transfer to the Recipient Institution data as further described in Appendix 3 (“Data”) collected as part of the [REDACTED].

flags.clause_title: 9. The liability of either party for any breach of this Agreement, or arising in any
flags.snippet: 9. The liability of either party for any breach of this Agreement, or arising in any other way out of the subject matter of this Agreement, will not extend to loss of business or profit, or to any indirect or consequential damages or losses.
flags.rationale: This clause explicitly excludes liability for indirect or consequential losses, as well as loss of business or profit, which aligns with the UoA Preferred Position for Liability Limitations and Exclusions (POS-07). The clause does not introduce any conflicting elements such as uncapped liability or 'hold harmless' provisions, and no monetary cap is required for this exclusion to be compliant.
flags.text:
9. The liability of either party for any breach of this Agreement, or arising in any other
way out of the subject matter of this Agreement, will not extend to loss of business
or profit, or to any indirect or consequential damages or losses.

flags.clause_title: 11. The Parties will aim to co-author any publications arising from this Agreement.
flags.snippet: 11. The Parties will aim to co-author any publications arising from this Agreement. For publications arising from use of the Material and/or Data not authored by the Provider Institution, the Recipient Scientist will acknowledge the Provider Institution as the source of the Material and/or Data and will provide the Provider Institution with a copy of any publications describing work carried out using the Material and/or Data.
flags.rationale: This clause preserves the right to publish by allowing co-authorship, acknowledgment of the Provider Institution, and provision of a copy of publications. It aligns with UoA's Preferred Position on publication rights, which includes co-authoring, acknowledgment, and reasonable review periods for confidential information. The clause does not impose unreasonable restrictions on publication and ensures academic freedoms are respected.
flags.text:
11. The Parties will aim to co-author any publications arising from this Agreement. For
publications arising from use of the Material and/or Data not authored by the
Provider Institution, the Recipient Scientist will acknowledge the Provider
Institution as the source of the Material and/or Data and will provide the Provider
Institution with a copy of any publications describing work carried out using the
Material and/or Data. If the Recipient Scientist wishes to include in a publication
any information which has been provided by the Provider Institution with the
Material and which was clearly marked as “confidential” and “proprietary” at the
point of disclosure (“Confidential Information”), the Recipient Scientist must obtain

Provider Institution, the Recipient Institution shall provide the Provider Institution
with a confidential copy of the findings of the Study.

flags.clause_title: 13. The Recipient Institution shall keep all information relating to the Material and
flags.snippet: 13. The Recipient Institution shall keep all information relating to the Material and Data confidential and shall not use it for any purpose other than as provided in this Agreement. This shall not apply to information …
flags.rationale: The clause aligns with UoA Position #POS-11 on Confidentiality. It excludes information already public, independently developed, previously known by the receiving party, and information legally required to be disclosed, which matches the Preferred tier. While the clause does not explicitly mention UoA's ability to disclose to agents and Affiliates or a specific duration, these omissions do not materially deviate from the Preferred tier for this contract type.
flags.text:
13. The Recipient Institution shall keep all information relating to the Material and Data
confidential and shall not use it for any purpose other than as provided in this
Agreement. This shall not apply to information to the extent it is in the public
domain, was or becomes known without any obligation of confidence from a source
other than the Provider Institution, was independently developed by the Recipient
Institution or is required to be disclosed by law.

flags.clause_title: 18. This Agreement shall not be amended except in writing by duly authorised
flags.snippet: 18. This Agreement shall not be amended except in writing by duly authorised signatories of the Parties hereto.
flags.rationale: The clause aligns with the UoA Preferred Contracting Position POS-21 on 'Variations and Extensions,' which requires all variations to be negotiated and executed in writing prior to expiry of the current Agreement. This clause matches the preferred tier as it ensures amendments are formalised in writing by authorised signatories.
flags.text: 18. This Agreement shall not be amended except in writing by duly authorised signatories of the Parties hereto.

flags.clause_title: 23. Neither party shall use the names or trademarks of the other party or of any
flags.snippet: 23. Neither party shall use the names or trademarks of the other party or of any of the other party's affiliated entities in any advertising, publicity, endorsement, or promotion unless the other party has provided prior written consent for the particular use contemplated.
flags.rationale: This clause aligns with UoA Position #POS-14 on Publicity, which states that neither party can use names or brands of the other or their personnel without prior written consent. The clause explicitly requires prior written consent for any use of names or trademarks, and therefore matches the Preferred tier.
flags.text: 23. Neither party shall use the names or trademarks of the other party or of any of the other party's affiliated entities in any advertising, publicity, endorsement, or promotion unless the other party has provided prior written consent for the particular use contemplated. The terms of this section survive the termination, expiration, non-renewal, or rescission of this Agreement.
